text: Agile methodology is a popular approach to project management, especially in software development. Instead of finishing a whole project at once, teams work in short cycles called *sprints*, which usually last two to four weeks. Each sprint focuses on completing a set of tasks from a *backlog*, a prioritized list of work. The team holds daily *standups*—quick meetings to discuss progress and challenges. Work is broken down into *user stories*, which describe features from the user’s perspective. These stories help the team understand customer needs. Agile uses *iterative development*, meaning the product is improved in small steps based on feedback, making it flexible and responsive to change.
